steps.title: Determine what the word must mean in context
steps.content: The sentence says the fog was "obliterating traces of familiar landscapes." The verb "obliterating" means completely wiping out or making invisible. The surrounding context confirms this: the bridge had "become a spectral outline against the sky" — fog made it nearly invisible. So "traces" must mean the visible signs of those landscapes that fog can erase by obscuring them.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each substitute in the sentence "obliterating ______ of familiar landscapes":
  • Choice A — 'Debris': Debris means scattered physical wreckage. Fog cannot remove physical rubble — it only obscures what can be seen, not solid matter on the ground. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Copies': Copies means reproductions or duplicates. The passage describes actual landmarks like the bridge, not reproductions of them; there are no copies to obliterate. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Indications': Indications means visible signs or evidence of something. Fog obliterating the visible signs of familiar landscapes — the bridge outline, the riverbank — is exactly what the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Remembrances': Remembrances means memories or recollections. Fog is a physical phenomenon; it obscures what is visible but cannot erase mental memories. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
